attributes.content:
## Overview

The Playlist card gives the workspace a structured home for ordered collection of media items (audio, video) with playback controls and metadata.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Playlist usually evolves through drafting, review, and reuse. The card keeps its core content structured enough to be surfaced in different views, linked from related records, and updated by people or automation without losing provenance.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[AI Image]]** -- Playlist can incorporate generated visuals or treat image output as a linked asset.
- **[[Prompt Template]]** -- Prompt-driven drafting or summarization can turn Playlist into a reusable AI-assisted workflow.
- **[[Project]]** -- Playlist can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Task]]** -- Playlist often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Document]]** -- Playlist can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Brand Guide]]** -- Brand Guide can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Playlist.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Playlist as the structured home for ordered collection of media items (audio, video) with playback controls and metadata and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Playlist when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Playlist stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Playlist
cardInfo.summary: Ordered collection of media items (audio, video) with playback controls and metadata.
